content: I just got back from a work trip to Tokyo last week. The sushi at Tsukiji Market was unreal - I've been dreaming about it ever since.
That sounds amazing! I've heard the sushi there is on another level. Was this your first time in Japan?

Yeah, first time. I went for a UX conference at the Tokyo International Forum. Met some fascinating people from all over - a designer from Helsinki, a researcher from Singapore, and this really interesting guy from Brazil who does VR interfaces.
What a great networking opportunity! Did you get to explore much outside the conference?

I squeezed in a few days. Went to Akihabara for the tech shops - found this incredible 3D printer that I'm still thinking about buying. Also did a day trip to Hakone to see Mt. Fuji. The view was breathtaking but the cable car ride terrified me - I'm not great with heights.
I can imagine that cable car would be intense with such a view! Did you try any of the famous onsen hot springs while you were there?

Oh yeah, I went to one in Hakone. It was my first time and I was super nervous about the etiquette, but it was actually really relaxing after the initial awkwardness. I'm back in Phoenix now, working remotely from my apartment with the mountain view. Much prefer this to the Bay Area chaos I left behind last year.
Phoenix sounds like a nice change of pace. Do you miss anything about the Bay Area?

Honestly, not much. The coffee shops were great, but Phoenix has some solid spots too. I've been getting into pour-over brewing at home - found this amazing Ethiopian Yirgacheffe that's become my daily ritual. And the rent is so much better here. I'm actually saving up for a trip to Costa Rica next spring - heard the coffee farms there are incredible.
Costa Rica sounds perfect! Are you planning to visit any specific coffee regions?

Yeah, Tarrazú is on the list for sure. Also hoping to do some hiking - heard the cloud forests are amazing. Though I should probably work on my tennis game first, I've got a tournament coming up with some friends from my old San Francisco club. They're coming to visit next month and want to play at the Phoenix Tennis Center.
That tournament sounds fun! Are you feeling prepared for it?

Honestly? Not really. I've been so busy with work and planning this Costa Rica trip that I haven't practiced much. My backhand is terrible right now. But it'll be good to see everyone - we used to play every weekend back in SF. One of them, Marcus, just started a VR gaming company and he's been talking about this crazy new archery VR game he's developing.
Sounds like you have a really interesting group of friends! That VR archery game sounds fascinating - maybe something to try when you're in Costa Rica?
